 {"id":"brenner_bot-0fs","title":"Task: Create GitHub Actions workflow for tests","description":"Create CI workflow to run tests on every PR.

## Workflow Configuration
- Trigger on pull_request and push to main
- Run unit tests with Vitest
- Run E2E tests with Playwright
- Report coverage
- Cache dependencies

## Steps
1. Checkout code
2. Setup Bun
3. Install dependencies
4. Run lint
5. Run type-check
6. Run unit tests
7. Run E2E tests
8. Upload coverage report
9. Upload test artifacts

## Requirements
- Fast feedback (target: <5 minutes for unit tests)
- Clear failure messages
- Artifacts preservation for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:15.780691552-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.472532836-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.472532836-05:00","close_reason":"Created .github/workflows/test.yml with lint, unit tests, build, E2E, typecheck, and CLI test jobs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-djz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.048935463-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0b5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.150541808-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xfs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.183709782-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0kp","title":"Create first-time user onboarding flow","description":"# Task

Design and implement an onboarding experience for first-time visitors.

# The Problem

Users arriving at brennerbot.org need to quickly understand:
1. What is this? (A research tool based on Sydney Brenner's method)
2. Who was Brenner? (Brief bio)
3. Why does this matter? (Value proposition)
4. What can I do here? (Key actions)

# Onboarding Elements

## 1. Hero Section (already planned)
- Animated quotes give a taste of Brenner's voice
- Clear value proposition

## 2. 'What Is This?' Section (NEW)
A brief, scannable explanation:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  WHAT IS BRENNER BOT?                                        │
│                                                              │
│  Sydney Brenner (1927-2019) was one of the most successful  │
│  experimental biologists in history. But his METHOD is more  │
│  valuable than any single discovery.                         │
│                                                              │
│  This project reverse-engineers his cognitive architecture   │
│  and makes it executable for AI-assisted research.           │
│                                                              │
│  [📖 Read the Corpus]  [🔬 Explore the Method]               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 3. Quick Tour Option
- 'Take a quick tour' button
- Step-through overlay highlighting key sections
- Can be dismissed / 'Don't show again'

## 4. Guided Entry Points
Instead of just 'Browse Corpus', offer guided paths:
- 'New to Brenner? Start here →' (curated intro excerpts)
- 'Compare AI syntheses →' (distillations comparison)
- 'Ready to research? Start a session →'

# Implementation

## Tour Library
Use something like `react-joyride` or `intro.js` for the guided tour.

## Persistence
Store 'tour completed' in localStorage to not show again.

## Mobile
Simpler onboarding on mobile - maybe just the 'What Is This?' section without overlay tour.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 'What Is This?' section explains the project
- [ ] Optional tour available for new users
- [ ] Guided entry points help users choose their path
- [ ] Tour state persists (don't show again)
- [ ] Works on mobile","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHill","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:34.53227297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:49:53.182058057-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:49:53.182058057-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented WhatIsThis onboarding section with guided entry points and localStorage persistence","labels":["onboarding","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-9o2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.972337943-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hv6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:07.002380154-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ggv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:27:49.010916091-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-0lff","title":"TASK: Define Session-Hypothesis Containment Model","description":"
## Clarifying the Relationship

A Brenner Loop SESSION contains one or more HYPOTHESES. But this relationship isn't explicit in our data models.

### The Questions
1. Can a session have multiple active hypotheses?
2. Can a hypothesis belong to multiple sessions?
3. How do competing hypotheses within a session relate?
4. What happens to hypotheses when session closes?

### Proposed Model

#### One-to-Many: Session → Hypotheses
```typescript
interface Session {
  id: string;
  // ... other fields
  
  // A session contains multiple hypotheses
  hypotheses: {
    primary: string;      // The main hypothesis ID
    alternatives: string[]; // Competing hypothesis IDs
    archived: string[];   // Discarded/superseded hypothesis IDs
  };
}

interface HypothesisCard {
  id: string;
  sessionId: string;  // Belongs to exactly one session
  
  // Relationship to other hypotheses in same session
  relationship?: {
    type: 'alternative_to' | 'refinement_of' | 'supersedes';
    relatedId: string;
  };
}
```

#### Session-Hypothesis Lifecycle
```
Session Created
     │
     ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Primary Hypo    │ ◄─── Initial hypothesis
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼ (user adds competing hypothesis)
┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│ Primary Hypo    │ ◄──►│ Alternative 1   │
└────────┬────────┘     └────────┬────────┘
         │                       │
         └───────────┬───────────┘
                     │
                     ▼ (testing eliminates Alternative 1)
             ┌─────────────────┐
             │ Primary Hypo    │ ◄─── Winner
             │ (refined)       │
             └────────┬────────┘
                      │
                      ▼
              Session Synthesis
```

### Key Behaviors

#### Adding Competing Hypothesis
```typescript
async function addCompetingHypothesis(
  sessionId: string,
  competingWith: string,
  newHypothesis: Partial<HypothesisCard>
): Promise<HypothesisCard> {
  // Create new hypothesis linked to session
  const hypo = await createHypothesis({
    ...newHypothesis,
    sessionId,
    relationship: {
      type: 'alternative_to',
      relatedId: competingWith,
    }
  });
  
  // Update session to track alternative
  await updateSession(sessionId, {
    hypotheses: {
      ...session.hypotheses,
      alternatives: [...session.hypotheses.alternatives, hypo.id]
    }
  });
  
  return hypo;
}
```

#### Resolving Competition
```typescript
async function resolveCompetition(
  sessionId: string,
  winnerId: string,
  loserId: string,
  reason: string
): Promise<void> {
  // Archive the loser
  await updateHypothesis(loserId, {
    state: 'superseded',
    supersededBy: winnerId,
    supersededReason: reason,
  });
  
  // Update session
  await updateSession(sessionId, {
    hypotheses: {
      primary: winnerId,
      alternatives: session.hypotheses.alternatives.filter(id => id !== loserId),
      archived: [...session.hypotheses.archived, loserId]
    }
  });
}
```

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/sessionHypothesis.ts`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session data model includes hypothesis relationships
- [ ] Can add competing hypotheses to session
- [ ] Can resolve competition (winner/loser)
- [ ] Archived hypotheses preserved for learning

### Depends On
- 1v26.1 (Session Data Model)
- an1n.1 (HypothesisCard Interface)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0li","title":"Create distillations overview page with model cards","description":"# Task

Build the /distillations overview page that introduces all three model syntheses.

# Page Structure

1. **Header** - 'The Three Distillations' with brief intro
2. **Model Cards** - One card per model (Opus, GPT, Gemini)
3. **Comparison CTA** - Link to comparison view

# Model Card Content

Each card shows:
- Model name + logo/icon
- Signature color (Opus: orange, GPT: blue, Gemini: cyan)
- Metaphor ('Philosophy of Science' / 'Decision Theory' / 'Operating System')
- Core question it answers
- 2-3 unique contributions
- 'Read Full' button

# Layout

- 3-column grid on desktop
- Single column on mobile
- Cards have equal height

# Why This First

Users need an entry point to understand the distillations before diving into comparison. This page provides that orientation.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Three model cards render correctly
- [ ] Each card has correct content
- [ ] Links to individual distillation pages work
- [ ] Comparison CTA visible
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:20:22.627743856-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:20.05337566-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:20.05337566-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: /distillations page has model cards, comparison table, convergences section, reading guide, responsive layout","labels":["distillations","overview","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:00.256561797-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-igw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:02.93339356-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:28.455279492-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:28.508908397-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0meb","title":"Task: Core Library Unit Tests (No Mocks)","description":"# Goal
Add unit tests for all untested library modules in `apps/web/src/lib/`.

# Modules to Test

## 1. corpus.ts (8.2KB)
Test corpus configuration and data loading:
- Document metadata retrieval
- Category filtering
- Document path resolution
- Corpus index generation

## 2. prompt-builder.ts (7.8KB)
Test prompt composition:
- Template variable substitution
- Excerpt injection
- Role prompt assembly
- Token counting/truncation

## 3. prompts.ts (2.2KB)
Test prompt templates:
- Template string validity
- Variable placeholder presence
- Default value handling

## 4. search/engine.ts
Test search engine core:
- Query parsing
- Index building
- Result ranking
- Fuzzy matching

## 5. search/hooks.ts
Test search React hooks:
- useSearch state management
- Debounced search
- Result caching
- Error state handling

## 6. operators.ts + operators.local.ts
Test operator definitions:
- Operator lookup by symbol
- Category filtering
- Description retrieval
- Local operator overrides

# Test Pattern
```typescript
// corpus.test.ts
import { getCorpusDocument, getCorpusIndex } from './corpus';

describe('corpus', () => {
  describe('getCorpusDocument', () => {
    it('returns document metadata for valid doc ID', () => {
      const doc = getCorpusDocument('transcript-batch-1');
      expect(doc).toMatchObject({
        id: 'transcript-batch-1',
        type: 'transcript',
        title: expect.any(String),
      });
    });
    
    it('returns null for unknown doc ID', () => {
      expect(getCorpusDocument('nonexistent')).toBeNull();
    });
  });
});
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] corpus.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] prompt-builder.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] prompts.test.ts with 100% coverage (small file)
- [ ] search/engine.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] search/hooks.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] operators.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] All tests use real corpus data, no mock documents","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkLake","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:50:42.137578449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:17:03.729511182-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:17:03.729511182-05:00","close_reason":"All 5 core library test files created/verified: prompts.test.ts, corpus.test.ts, prompt-builder.test.ts, search/engine.test.ts, search/hooks.test.ts. 1370 tests pass.","labels":["testing unit-tests lib"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0meb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:16.082494539-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
